metadata.evaluator_organization: University of Alberta (Department of Computing Science)

evaluation_inputs.system_description: An Event framework designed to handle and process events for an event-driven application. The framework consists of an Event Manager, Event Queue, and Handlers. The application utilizes these services to process user inputs from a command-line interface, binding events to specific handlers via a registry.

phase_1_discovery.initial_documentation_status: Fully developed architecture with class diagrams, component descriptions, and defined component interactions.
evaluator_inquiry_questions: Can the components of the architecture be reused for future projects?
Can the framework be expanded in the future to accommodate a new application or new components?
Will the system handle any input given by the user and handle invalid input?
Is the architecture consistent in behavior?
Can any new application-specific functionality be added to the architecture?
Can the system be modified in short time and cost-effective manner?
Does the architecture perform its event-processing task properly?

hardware_view_description: System accepts input via Command Line Interface (CLI)
Single processor execution implied (sequential event processing)
runtime_constraints: Events must be dispatched in a First-Come-First-Served (FIFO) pattern
System must maintain an 'idle wait' state until input is received
Invalid inputs must be handled gracefully

quantitative_model_parameters.performance_proxy: Number of components involved in a transaction
quantitative_model_parameters.transaction_speed: Qualitative (Fastest possible based on minimal component interaction)

questions: Does the system allow unauthorized access?
Does the architecture portray data confidentiality?
Does the architecture process any task in the fastest possible speed?

raw_candidate_scenarios.description: No unauthorized access to the system
All operations are processed in fastest possible speed
Any failure should be followed by immediate recovery
User errors in using system should be handled
New demands on system functionality should be accommodated
Major parts of the framework should be re-usable for future architectures
Framework modification is cost-effective, fast and in minimal time
Components in framework interact in a coordinated manner
Framework expansion is possible to allow for more sophisticated options
Implementation allows for execution in different environments
Proper data encapsulation and secure data structures
Flexible implementation using other programming languages
Overall consistent behavior expected from the architecture
Framework should be complete, clear and perform exactly as required
Hook up multiple applications to the framework at same time
Change interface from command-driven to window-based

business_goals: Provide an Event framework to be used by an application
Handle and process events through component interaction
Allow application components to utilize the framework services
technical_constraints: Must handle system termination via STOP handler
Must maintain system in idle state until input is received

architectural_approaches.description: The framework components (Event Manager, Queue, Event Type Registry) are separated from the application. The Event Manager binds events to application-dependent handlers at runtime using a registry.

addressed_attributes.rationale: Application is outside the framework; components can be singled out and reused.
Clear-cut hooks visible to the application builder allow expansion.
Flawed input is handled at the Event Manager level.

scenarios.scenario_text: Components in framework interact in a coordinated manner
Overall consistent behavior expected from the architecture
Framework should be complete, clear and perform exactly as required
User errors in using system should be handled
New demands on system functionality should be accommodated
No unauthorized access to the system
All operations are processed in fastest possible speed
Major parts of the framework should be re-usable for future architectures

scenario_reference.text: Can the framework be expanded in the future to accommodate a new application or new components?

analysis_records.stimulus: Request to add new components or restructure
architectural_decisions: Application is not embedded in framework components
Event Manager maintains an event-type registry
analysis_records.reasoning: The framework is highly flexible because the application is not embedded in many components. Any component can be restructured or added without affecting others.

non_risks.rationale: Framework components are independent; new schemes can be added without restructuring the whole system.

scenario_reference.text: Will the system handle any input given by the user and handle invalid input?

analysis_records.stimulus: Invalid input entered at command line
architectural_decisions: Input handling without elimination of flawed input at source
Event Manager handles flawed input binding
analysis_records.reasoning: Although flawed input propagates to the event binding time, it is eventually handled properly at the Event Manager component.

non_risks.rationale: Flawed input is handled in a proper manner at the event manager component.

scenario_reference.text: Is the architecture consistent in behavior?

architectural_decisions: Standardized event handling path (Main -> Manager -> Handler)
analysis_records.reasoning: Regardless of event type, the process is handled in a similar, efficient manner with minimal control mechanisms.

non_risks.rationale: Consistent handling of events with minimal control mechanisms.

scenario_reference.text: Can any new application-specific functionality be added to the architecture?

architectural_decisions: Event Manager uses dynamic Event Type Registry
analysis_records.reasoning: Since the application is not embedded in framework components, modifications can be done cost-effectively.

non_risks.rationale: Application is added at clearly defined hooks; contents of registry are dependent on application, not fixed.

sensitivity_points.parameter: Number of places where application is embedded

scenario_reference.text: Does the system allow unauthorized access?

analysis_records.reasoning: Only the Event Manager does the event registry activity; unauthorized components cannot manipulate the registry data structure.

non_risks.rationale: Proper data encapsulation ensures information is visible only to components that need it.

scenario_reference.text: Does the architecture process any task in the fastest possible speed?

analysis_records.reasoning: The number of components involved in performing any task is minimal.

non_risks.rationale: Time taken to perform one transaction is minimal due to efficient interaction.

sensitivity_points.parameter: Number of components involved in processing
